meta.description: Australian federal candidate transparency dataset (48th Parliament). Roster from AEC Members Elected (House) and OpenAustralia (Senate); donor data from AEC Member of Parliament returns. Policy positions are added separately with their own sources. See ../CONTRIBUTING.md.

donors.summary: Donations disclosed directly to this member in AEC Member of Parliament returns. Note: member returns capture only donations made directly to the member, not money received via a party; figures are partial.

sources.title: AEC Member of Parliament annual return — Ms Alicia Payne MP (2020-21)

faith.summary: Andrew Leigh is a self-described atheist; he has written and spoken publicly about being an atheist while arguing that churches benefit society.

sources.title: The Atheist Who Wants More Churches in Australia
The church is good for social connection, says federal politician and atheist

faith.summary: Husic was born to Bosnian Muslim immigrant parents and is the first Muslim elected to Australia's federal parliament; his household practised Islam while also celebrating Christmas and Easter, and after the September 11 attacks he described himself as a 'non-practising' Muslim. He took his ministerial oath on the Quran.

sources.title: Ed Husic

faith.summary: Leeser is Jewish; he is described as the first Jewish Liberal member of the House of Representatives from New South Wales and a member of the Emanuel Synagogue in Woollahra. He announced he would wear a kippah in federal parliament for one week as a statement against rising antisemitism in Australia.

sources.title: Julian Leeser

faith.summary: Plibersek has said she was raised Catholic. In her 2022 Archbishop Daniel Mannix Lecture she said 'I was raised firmly within the arms of the Catholic Church,' describing her first communion, confirmation, Sunday mass and weekly confession, and adding that 'growing up Catholic has influenced my politics.' She does not state whether she currently practises.

sources.title: Speech – Tanya Plibersek – The 38th Archbishop Daniel Mannix Lecture – Melbourne – Wednesday, 23 March 2022

faith.summary: Josh Burns is Jewish. His maternal grandmother came to Australia as a Jewish refugee from Nazi Germany, he was educated at Mt Scopus Memorial College (a Jewish day school) and was active in the Jewish Labour Bund youth movement; he has spoken publicly as a Jewish person and is categorised among Jewish members of the Australian parliament.

sources.title: Josh Burns (politician)

faith.summary: Sam Lim is a practising Buddhist; he became the first person to be sworn into the Australian federal parliament on Buddhist scripture (the Dhammapada), and has said Buddhism has 'always been a positive force' in his life giving him 'great inner strength'. The Federation of Australian Buddhist Councils describes him as 'perhaps the first Buddhist Federal Member of Parliament'.

sources.title: 'I still get lost in Parliament House': Australia's second Malaysian-born federal MP discusses the road ahead
We congratulate Sam Lim on being elected to Federal Parliament

faith.summary: In her first (maiden) speech to the Senate, Smith referred to "my Christian values of social justice and fairness," saying she found a political home in the Labor Party because of those values. This is a self-identification of Christian values rather than a stated denomination or practising status.

sources.title: First Speech - Marielle Smith
